description: baseline
Increase WARMDOWN_RATIO from 0.5 to 0.75 for more aggressive LR cooldown
Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine decay schedule
Increase weight decay on embeddings and value embeddings from 0.001 to 0.003
Add label smoothing (0.1) to cross entropy loss to improve generalization and reduce overfitting
Increase logits softcap from 12 to 30 for higher dynamic range

Increase weight decay on lm_head (unembedding) from 0.001 to 0.003 to match the embedding weight decay increase that previously improved val_bpb. The lm_head shares weights with embeddings but was excluded from the previous weight decay adjustment.
Add 0.1 dropout to attention and MLP residual paths for regularization
Add 0.1 dropout to token embeddings for additional regularization, matching the existing residual dropout rate
Increase z-loss coefficient from 1e-4 to 1e-3 to strengthen logit regularization and reduce overconfident predictions
Use full attention in all layers by changing window pattern to LLLL
Increase logits softcap from 12 to 15 for improved dynamic range and gradient flow
Increase weight decay on embedding, value embedding, and lm_head parameter groups from 0.001 to 0.003 to combine the improvements from previous experiments
Increase logits softcap to 50 for higher dynamic range
Initialize x0_lambdas to 0.0 instead of 0.1 to reduce initial skip-connection contribution from the input embedding, simplifying early training dynamics
Reduce Muon optimizer ns_steps from 5 to 3 to improve training throughput (more steps in 5 min)
Change RoPE base from 50000 to 10000 for standard frequency scaling
Replace ReLU squared activation with GeLU in MLP for improved gradient flow
Increase logits softcap from 12 to 20 for moderate dynamic range increase between baseline and successful softcap-30 experiment
Change window pattern from SSSL to SSSS to increase training throughput (more short-window layers = less compute per step, allowing more steps in 5min budget)
Increase z-loss coefficient from 1e-4 to 2e-4 for slightly stronger logit regularization
Add 0.1 dropout to MLP activation output (after ReLU square, before projection) for additional regularization
Add 0.1 dropout to value embeddings (ve) before the gating mechanism to regularize the value residual stream
Add 0.1 dropout to attention weights via dropout_p parameter in both SDPA and flash_attn_func to improve regularization
Add 0.1 dropout to the x0 residual connection (initial embedding skip) in each layer to regularize the deep residual pathway
Add 0.1 dropout to queries and keys after rotary embedding and normalization for regularization
Increase weight decay on resid_lambdas from 0.001 to 0.003 for stronger regularization of per-layer residual scales
Increase weight decay on x0_lambdas from 0.0 to 0.001 to regularize deep residual skip-connection scalars
Add 0.1 dropout to attention and MLP residual paths and remove weight decay on resid_lambdas to reduce regularization on per-layer residual scales
Reduce z-loss coefficient from 1e-4 to 5e-5 to decrease regularization on logit magnitudes, potentially allowing better convergence with the existing dropout regularization.
Add 0.1 dropout to attention and MLP residual paths and increase logits softcap from 12 to 30
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability to residual connections in transformer blocks
Use layer-wise linearly increasing DropPath probability (stochastic depth) from 0.05 in shallow layers to 0.15 in deep layers instead of uniform 0.1

Apply stochastic depth (DropPath) regularization with 0.1 drop probability only to the MLP residual connection, leaving the attention residual path unchanged to isolate regularization on the MLP sub-block
Add 0.1 dropout to the final hidden state (output of the last layer norm) before the language modeling head to regularize the representation used for next-token prediction

Maintain exponential moving average (EMA) of model parameters with decay 0.9999 and use the EMA weights for final validation evaluation. This provides a more stable model for inference by averaging parameters over training.
Add a 10% linear LR warmup at the start of training to stabilize early updates

Implement scheduled DropPath (stochastic depth) with linear warmup from 0.0 to 0.2 drop probability over the training budget, applied to both attention and MLP residual connections. This extends the successful constant 0.1 DropPath experiment by gradually increasing regularization strength as training progresses. [timeout (900s wall clock)]
Use grouped query attention (GQA) by halving n_kv_head from 6 to 3 to reduce memory bandwidth and compute in attention, allowing more training steps within the 5-minute budget

Apply DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability only to the attention residual connection, leaving the MLP residual path unchanged. This applies Bernoulli dropout to the attention output (scaled by 1/0.9 during training) before adding to the residual stream, while keeping the MLP residual connection intact.
Combine stochastic depth (DropPath) with 0.1 probability on residual connections and light dropout (0.05) on the final hidden state before the language modeling head to improve generalization
Untie input/output embeddings (wte and lm_head) to fix duplicate parameter in optimizer groups and initialize lm_head separately
Add Gaussian noise (std=0.01) to input embeddings during training for regularization
Replace cross-entropy loss with focal loss (gamma=1.0) to focus training on hard-to-predict tokens and down-weight easy examples, which may improve generalization on the long-tail token distribution.

Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.05 drop probability to residual connections in transformer blocks. This tests a lower dropout rate than the previous best of 0.1 to check if less regularization improves generalization.
Use full attention in the last two transformer layers instead of just the final layer to increase receptive field for global context aggregation without significantly impacting training speed

Use layer-wise linearly decreasing DropPath probability (stochastic depth) from 0.15 in shallow layers to 0.05 in deep layers (inverse of previous increasing schedule) to test if regularizing early layers more heavily improves generalization
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 probability to residual connections and apply 0.05 dropout to the final hidden state before the language modeling head to improve generalization
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability to residual connections in transformer blocks to improve generalization
Use a single shared x0_lambda scalar across all layers instead of per-layer parameters to reduce overfitting and improve generalization

Add LayerScale (learnable residual scaling) with initialization 0.1 to attention and MLP outputs in each transformer block to stabilize training of deep residual networks [Traceback (most recent call last):
Add bias terms to MLP layers (c_fc and c_proj) to improve optimization capacity and training stability [Traceback (most recent call last):
Reduce torch.compile compilation overhead by setting fullgraph=False to allow graph breaks, enabling faster startup and more training steps within the 5-minute budget
Fix MLP residual connection bug: remove internal residual from MLP class since Block already adds residual, preventing double residual addition
Combine best regularization techniques: Add DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 probability to residual connections AND add 0.1 dropout to final hidden state before lm_head

Change the attention window pattern from 'SSSL' to 'SLSS' to place the long-range attention layer earlier in the block cycle, potentially improving gradient flow through earlier long-range dependencies
Disable value embeddings (ResFormer) to reduce computation and increase training throughput, allowing more gradient steps within the 5-minute budget [Traceback (most recent call last):

Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability to the value embedding residual connection (the addition of gated value embeddings to attention values) to regularize the ResFormer value residual pathway
Add 0.1 dropout to MLP c_proj output (before internal residual addition) to regularize the MLP sub-block output
Enable cuDNN benchmarking to automatically select fastest algorithms for the fixed input shapes, improving training throughput and allowing more gradient steps within the 5-minute budget
Add learnable bias to value embedding gate (ve_gate) and initialize to zero for improved gating flexibility [Traceback (most recent call last):
Replace MLP with SwiGLU activation (gated linear unit with SiLU) adjusting hidden dim to 8/3*n_embd to maintain similar parameter count while improving gradient flow and representational capacity

Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability to transformer block residual connections. This randomly drops the residual branches during training (scaled by 1/(1-0.1)) to improve generalization, matching the best previous result.
Add 0.05 dropout to MLP input after normalization for targeted regularization of the feedforward sub-block

Reduce sliding window size for 'S' layers from half to quarter sequence length (512 instead of 1024) to increase training throughput, allowing more gradient steps within the 5-minute budget while maintaining full attention in the final layer for long-range dependencies
Reduce Value Embedding layers to last 2 only (from alternating 6 layers) to improve training throughput, allowing more gradient steps within the 5-minute budget
Combine DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 probability and 0.05 dropout on MLP input after normalization to improve generalization beyond the current best
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.09 drop probability to residual connections in transformer blocks
Use parallel attention and MLP (PaLM-style) with shared pre-normalization to improve throughput by reducing normalization operations from 2 to 1 per block and allowing parallel computation

Apply asymmetric DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.05 drop probability to attention residual and 0.15 to MLP residual, based on the observation that MLP benefits from stronger regularization than attention in this architecture

Combine DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 drop probability on both attention and MLP residual connections, and add 0.05 dropout to the MLP input after normalization. This combines two complementary regularization techniques from near-miss experiments to improve generalization. [Traceback (most recent call last):
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 probability to the MLP internal residual connection, randomly dropping the MLP transformation output during training to regularize the feedforward sub-block

Initialize value embedding gate weights to -2.0 instead of 0.0 to start with weaker gating (sigmoid(-2)*2≈0.24) and let the model gradually learn to incorporate value embeddings rather than having them fully active from the start
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability to residual connections in transformer blocks to improve generalization
Increase AdamW beta1 from 0.8 to 0.9 for embedding and head parameters to provide stronger momentum for sparse gradient updates
Reduce MLP expansion ratio from 4x to 3x to decrease model size and increase training throughput, allowing more gradient steps within the 5-minute budget
Change attention window pattern from 'SSSL' to 'LSSS' to place long-range attention at the beginning of the block cycle, potentially improving early global context aggregation.
Change MLP activation from ReLU squared to standard ReLU to reduce computational overhead and potentially improve gradient flow, allowing more training steps within the 5-minute budget
Add input token corruption regularization: randomly replace 1% of input token IDs with random vocabulary tokens during training to prevent overfitting and improve generalization
Add 0.05 dropout to attention values (v) after the value embedding residual connection to regularize the attention value stream without affecting query/key normalization

Apply DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 drop probability starting only after 20% training progress (delayed regularization), allowing faster initial convergence before applying strong regularization
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 drop probability to residual connections in transformer blocks to improve generalization
Initialize resid_lambdas to 0.9 instead of 1.0 to weaken initial residual connections, acting as a deterministic form of stochastic depth that encourages shallower feature reuse early in training
Reduce value embedding gate channels from 32 to 16 to decrease gating capacity and increase regularization
Fix MLP double residual bug and add DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 probability to residual connections
Add LayerDrop (stochastic layer dropping) with 0.1 probability to randomly skip transformer block computation during training, improving throughput for more steps and regularizing via depth variation
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 drop probability to residual connections in transformer blocks

Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability to residual connections in even-indexed transformer layers only (layers 0, 2, 4, ...) to test if regularizing alternating layers improves generalization over uniform application
Increase value embedding gate channels from 32 to 64 to enhance gating capacity for the ResFormer value residual stream
Maintain non-zero learning rate at end of training by setting FINAL_LR_FRAC to 0.1 instead of 0.0 to prevent premature convergence
Add scheduled Gaussian noise to gradients (std=0.0001*(1-progress)) after backward to improve generalization and escape sharp minima

Share value embeddings (ResFormer) across all transformer layers instead of having separate embeddings per layer. This reduces parameter count and acts as a regularization mechanism by forcing all layers to use the same value residual representation.
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability to residual connections in transformer blocks to improve generalization
Reduce Muon momentum warmup duration from 500 to 200 steps to reach optimal momentum faster, allowing more training steps at high momentum within the 5-minute budget

Apply attention temperature scaling by multiplying queries by 1.25 after normalization to sharpen the attention distribution, encouraging more focused attention patterns and potentially improving generalization.
Align value embeddings optimizer betas with main embeddings by changing from (0.9, 0.999) to adam_betas (0.8, 0.95) for improved optimization consistency

Add DropPath (stochastic depth) regularization with 0.1 drop probability to the x0 residual connection (the per-layer addition of the initial embedding skip connection) to regularize the deep residual pathway
Add entropy regularization to the loss with coefficient 0.001 to encourage smoother output distributions and prevent overconfident predictions
Add DropPath (stochastic depth) with 0.1 probability to residual connections and apply light 0.01 dropout to MLP input after normalization to improve generalization beyond the current best
Disable z-loss regularization by zeroing its coefficient to reduce computational overhead and allow more gradient steps within the 5-minute budget
